name: ParticleFXEmitter
shortDescription: Advanced 2D & 3D particle system with keyframe animations, supporting Shape Painter and 3D scenes.
description: ## Overview
Complete particle system for GDevelop with **2D** (custom 2D object) and **3D** (custom 3D object) support. Design effects in the GDParticleFX Editor and import them via JSON.
## Features
- **Multiple emission modes**: infinite, burst, finite (reservoir)
- **Spawn zones**: point, circle, rectangle, line (2D) — sphere, box, line (3D)
- **Directional cone** with spread, rotation, and elevation (3D)
- **Per-keyframe easing**: linear, easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut
- **Particle shapes**: circle, square, triangle, star, diamond, line, custom SVG
- **3D shapes**: sphere, cube, diamond, billboard, custom GLB models, textured images
- **Runtime overrides**: flow, direction, spread, speed/size/opacity multipliers
- **Gravity** and **align to velocity** support
- **Visual debug guides** for cone and spawn zone
- **Reverse mode**: particles converge toward the emitter
## 2D — Custom Object
Works on any 2D layer. Supports Simple SVG, PixiJS Sprite rendered with PixiJS.
## 3D — Custom Object
Works on any 3D layer. Supports GLB models, textured billboards, and 3D primitives rendered with Three.js.
## Use Cases
- Explosions, fire, smoke, sparks, magic effects
- Rain, snow, confetti, bubbles, ambient particles
- Trail effects, UI highlights, celebrations
- 3D volumetric effects and model-based particles
## How to Use
1. Export your effect as JSON from GDParticleFX Editor
2. **2D**: Add the 2D emitter object to a D layer
3. **3D**: Add the 3D emitter object to a 3D layer
4. Set the ParticleJSON property — effect plays automatically!

eventsBasedObjects.description: Display particles with Particles FX Editor.
eventsBasedObjects.name: ParticleFXEmitter3D

console.warn("[GDParticleFX2D] Error loading GDevelop image resource: ", e);

else if (d < w+h)   { lx = w/2;                ly = (d-w) - h/2; }
else if (d < 2*w+h) { lx = w/2 - (d - w - h);  ly = h/2; }

// Custom shape: look up texture in cache by shape ID
gfx.userData = { type: "graphics", shape: shape, lastSize: -1, lastColor: -1, lastOpacity: -1 };
// Draw a primitive shape on a PIXI.Graphics object (with caching to skip redundant redraws)
F.drawShape = function(gfx, shape, size, color, opacity, starPoints, starInnerRatio) {
// Skip redraw if properties haven't changed significantly
if (ud && ud.lastSize === size && ud.lastColor === color && ud.lastOpacity === opacity) {
// Resolve group → pick a random shape from the group
// Try to get recycled particle from pool
// Dispose a particle display object (return to pool for recycling)
// Reset cached draw state for graphics objects
// Add to pool for recycling (keep pool size reasonable)
// Pool is full, destroy the object
// Get a particle from pool or create a new one
// Try to find a matching particle in the pool
// No match in pool, create new display object
var cA1 = cDir - cHalf, cA2 = cDir + cHalf;
data.pool = []; // Clear pool too on reset
// Position the containers at emitter pos
// Ensure guides always render on top of particles by moving to end of parent's display list
// --- Counter-transform: keep particles in world/layer coords inside the object's container ---
// The particleContainer lives inside the custom object's PIXI container.
// The object's container applies position, rotation, scale, pivot transforms.
// We undo those on the particleContainer so particles stay in world coordinates
// while inheriting Z-order, object effects, and layer effects automatically.
if (_renderer.ensureUpToDate) _renderer.ensureUpToDate(); // sync PIXI container to current frame
// Force local transform update, then compute inverse
// Decompose inverse matrix → rotation + skew + scale (exact)
if (directionOverride >= 0) emitter.cone.direction = directionOverride; else emitter.cone.direction = oc.direction;
if (spreadOverride >= 0) emitter.cone.spread = spreadOverride; else emitter.cone.spread = oc.spread;
if (flowOverride >= 0) emitter.flow = flowOverride; else emitter.flow = data._originalFlow;
// --- Update & render particles (using swap-and-pop for O(1) removal) ---
// Dead? Use swap-and-pop for O(1) removal
// Swap with last element and pop
// Shape transition: swap display object when keyframe shape changes
// If keyframe shape is 'group', skip transition — particle keeps its resolved shape from spawn
var newObj = F.createDisplayObject(needShape, needCustomId, fromState.size, data);
// Remove old display object without pooling (shape changed)
var size    = F.lerp(fromState.size, toState.size, t) * sizeMult;
var opacity = F.lerp(fromState.opacity, toState.opacity, t) * opacityMult;
var speed   = F.lerp(fromSpd, toSpd, t) * speedMult;
// Scale velocity to match interpolated speed
var curSpd = Math.sqrt(p.vx * p.vx + p.vy * p.vy);
// PIXI.Graphics — redraw shape (use resolved shape, not keyframe shape, for group particles)
F.drawShape(dObj, drawShape, size, colorHex, opacity, fromState.starPoints, fromState.starInnerRatio);
// --- Rebuild guides when relevant properties change ---
p.displayObj.destroy({ children: true, texture: false, baseTexture: false });
// --- Clear texture cache (don't destroy - may be shared) ---
